result.summary: Binary Authorization exposes a Google Cloud REST API at `https://binaryauthorization.googleapis.com` plus `gcloud` CLI commands under `gcloud beta container binauthz`; both use Google Cloud authentication with bearer access tokens.
result.description: Binary Authorization is a Google Cloud service for policy-based deployment validation and control of container images and related platform policies. It lets organizations define attestors and policies that govern what software can be deployed.

gcp_oauth_access_token.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth_access_token.setup: For REST and client-library access, authenticate to Google Cloud and obtain an access token as described in [Authenticate for using REST](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/rest). For local development, the easiest path is to set up Application Default Credentials from the [Google Cloud authentication guides](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication). For CLI use, sign in with the [gcloud CLI authentication flow](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/authenticate), typically via `gcloud auth login` or `gcloud auth application-default login`.
